summary: Backend testing for real Diagnostic Tools + MikroTik Torch + Auto-Block IP security. Created 20 new tests covering /admin/diagnostics/tools + /admin/diagnostics/run (ping, traceroute, dns, whois, blacklist, portscan, http, torch), torch validation & happy-path, RBAC, and end-to-end auto-block flow (12 fails -> 429 -> manual unblock). Found + fixed a critical timezone-comparison bug in _is_ip_blocked and _maybe_auto_block that was returning 500 on the 11th failed login. All 20 new tests + 13 recaptcha_auth + 13 login_analytics + 14 assets_straight_line = 60 tests green.

minor.issue: tools list contains 9 entries because 'nslookup' is a runtime alias for 'dns' — meta has 8 (matches review spec). Not a real defect but worth noting; test uses meta length + presence checks.

action_items: Consider persisting blocked_ips expires_at consistently: _maybe_auto_block writes datetime, DELETE /blocked-ips/{ip} writes ISO string via _now(). Downstream code now normalizes both, but a single format would be cleaner.
Optionally return a token-free health probe endpoint to ping the diagnostics service without an admin session (currently 401 for anonymous, which is correct for security).
critical_code_review_comments: BUG (FIXED) — /app/backend/portal/routes.py::_is_ip_blocked and _maybe_auto_block compared offset-naive datetimes returned by MongoDB against offset-aware datetime.now(timezone.utc). This produced 'TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes' on every subsequent /auth/login once a block had been recorded, causing a 500 instead of the intended 429. Normalized tzinfo before comparison (also handled string ISO form from _now()).
BUG (FIXED) — blocked_ips_list had the same tz-naive comparison on the `active` computation.

Consistency — _maybe_auto_block stores expires_at as python datetime while the DELETE unblock endpoint stores expires_at as an ISO string via _now(). Consider standardizing to ISO strings across the board.

Torch dispatcher signature: dispatch(tool, target, ...) calls run_torch(target=None, ...) which is correct, but run_torch signature is `async def run_torch(target: str | None = None, *, interface: str = '', ...)` — the empty-string default for interface means a completely missing 'interface' key doesn't reach the get_settings check; it fails validation first (interface is required). Order-of-validation is fine, but worth documenting: interface-validation happens BEFORE mikrotik-integration check, so bad-interface returns 400 even when mikrotik is disabled (test_12 accepts either error).
PORT-SCAN uses hardcoded COMMON_PORTS list; consider making it configurable via payload without opening arbitrary port scans (rate-limit or admin-only-only).
updated_files: /app/backend/portal/routes.py — fixed tz-naive vs tz-aware datetime comparisons in _is_ip_blocked, _maybe_auto_block, blocked_ips_list
/app/backend/tests/test_recaptcha_auth.py — added autouse fixture to disable auto_block during the class run
/app/backend/tests/test_login_analytics.py — added autouse fixture to disable auto_block during the class run
seed_data_creation: None. Uses existing seeded admin/client. Teardown wipes blocked_ips and 127.0.0.1 failed login_attempts + disables auto_block after the diagnostics test class ends.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-block is DISABLED by the diagnostics test teardown (and by the recaptcha/login-analytics test fixtures). If a future test needs auto-block ON, PUT /admin/security/settings with auto_block_enabled:true. Also, all 4 test files touching 127.0.0.1 login state are pinned to xdist_group='recaptcha_shared' so they serialize on one worker — do NOT change that pin.
rca of the issue: RCA: MongoDB's motor driver returns tz-naive datetimes even when tz-aware datetimes are written. _maybe_auto_block wrote `expires_at = now_dt + timedelta(...)` as a python datetime (tz-aware). On read, motor returns it stripped of tzinfo (naive UTC). _is_ip_blocked then did `expires_at > now_dt` (naive > aware) which raises TypeError. Because _is_ip_blocked runs at the top of /auth/login and is not wrapped in a try/except, the raw exception surfaced as a 500. Reproduction: (1) create fake IP block via 10 failed logins; (2) 11th /auth/login → 500. Mitigation: normalize `expires_at` (both str-ISO and naive-datetime variants) to tz-aware UTC before comparison in _is_ip_blocked, _maybe_auto_block, and blocked_ips_list.
